block_goal: Add a waiting list Django app and align existing server-rendered screens with the waiting_list.html visual and interaction model without changing the project stack.
architect_instructions_for_orchestrator: Run PM plan review before execution and do not dispatch any task until Architect confirms the reviewed plan.
Preserve unrelated uncommitted files in the worktree and keep each worker strictly inside the declared write scope.
Materialize task packets mechanically from the export spec without paraphrasing task content.
Use the declared runtime bindings as-is; if an external CLI worker is selected, route execution through the $cli-subagents workflow and wrappers instead of raw shell invocation.
Do not start a dependent slice until its prerequisites are accepted.
global_rules: Keep the UI stack server-rendered with Django Templates, HTMX, and shared CSS; do not introduce a new frontend framework or build pipeline.
Do not touch unrelated uncommitted files or revert existing user changes outside the declared task scopes.
Do not modify AI/runtime/tool-registry files in this block; the feature is bounded to product UI and a new Django app.
Use service-layer helpers for waiting list write actions so audit records stay server-enforced.
Update PROJECT_HANDOFF.md when the new app and shared UI conventions are materially introduced.
global_acceptance: The repository passes make check, make test, and make contracts at block completion.
Existing primary screens adopt the waiting_list-inspired visual system without breaking current routes, forms, or HTMX targets.
The new waiting list dashboard supports HTMX-driven filtering, sorting, drawer loading, and status updates without full-page reload.
The waiting list data model persists the required entry fields and timeline audit records while following repository PK conventions.
global_invariants: Django remains the system of record for all waiting list reads and writes.
The waiting list timeline is backed by a persisted audit-log model, not a client-side-only event list.
The block stays inside the monorepo's current Django app structure and shared static asset conventions.
Entry does not use UUID as primary key; the repository default int/BigAutoField convention remains in force.
If a non-guessable identifier becomes necessary, it must be a separate external_id UUID field rather than the primary key.

slices.title: Shared Waiting List Visual System
slices.goal: Move the waiting_list.html visual primitives into the shared base layout and CSS without changing application behavior.
forbidden_moves: Do not change route wiring or app registrations in this slice.
Do not remove the HTMX CSRF header or existing block structure from templates/base.html.
Do not break compatibility with the existing detail-panel drawer contract used by workorders.
control_points: Shared CSS tokens and primitives cover toolbar, table surface, badge, keyboard hint, and drawer foundations.
The base shell remains compatible with existing pages that extend base.html.
acceptance_criteria: The shared layout and CSS align with the waiting_list reference without introducing a new asset pipeline.
Existing pages still render under the shared shell after the foundation changes.

tasks.goal: Implement the shared visual system tokens, shell, and drawer primitives based on waiting_list.html.
implementation_notes: Keep the current navigation and HTMX hooks intact while replacing the visual language.
Expose reusable CSS primitives so later slices can restyle screens without redefining component styling.
Preserve backward-compatible selectors for workorder drawer behavior wherever practical.
deliverables: Updated shared CSS theme and components
escalate_if: A required base-layout change would break existing HTMX shell assumptions
Write scope must expand into application-specific Python modules

slices.title: Existing App Screen Adoption
slices.goal: Apply the shared waiting-list visual system to the current core product screens without changing domain logic.
forbidden_moves: Do not change business rules or model fields in any existing app.
Do not introduce new JavaScript infrastructure or client-side state management.
Do not broaden edits into AI app Python modules in this slice.
required_checks: ./.venv/bin/python manage.py test apps.core.tests apps.inventory.tests apps.workorders.tests apps.analytics.tests
Manual visual verification against drafts/waiting_list.html for shared layout alignment on desktop and mobile, including toolbar, table surfaces, badges, and drawer styling.
control_points: Dashboard, inventory, workorders, and analytics screens use the new shared components rather than ad hoc local styling.
Existing HTMX targets on the workorder board still resolve correctly after markup changes.
mandatory_tests: Update affected response/content tests if template structure changes invalidate current assertions.
acceptance_criteria: Existing primary screens visually align with the waiting list reference and still pass their current route-level tests.
No Python behavior outside template presentation changes in this slice.

tasks.goal: Restyle existing core, inventory, workorders, and analytics screens to the shared waiting-list visual language.
inputs: Existing Django templates for dashboard, inventory, workorders, and analytics
implementation_notes: Prefer shared component classes over one-off inline structure changes.
Keep current URLs, form field names, and HTMX attributes stable unless the new markup requires a bounded adjustment.
If drawer-related workorder partials need markup updates, keep the existing detail-panel semantics intact.
tests_to_write: Update existing tests in apps.core.tests, apps.inventory.tests, apps.workorders.tests, and apps.analytics.tests only where template expectations must change.
tests_to_run: ./.venv/bin/python manage.py test apps.core.tests apps.inventory.tests apps.workorders.tests apps.analytics.tests
deliverables: Adjusted shared CSS utilities if needed
escalate_if: A required restyle would force business-logic changes in Python modules
Existing HTMX interaction contracts cannot be preserved within the declared template scope

slices.title: Waiting List Domain And Routing
slices.goal: Add the new waiting list Django app, models, routes, service helpers, and migration in a bounded way.
forbidden_moves: Do not modify existing workorders or inventory schemas in this slice.
Do not add a separate service catalog model or a new permissions framework.
Do not wire direct client-side writes; all waiting list mutations must remain in Django views/services.
control_points: Entry follows the repository default int/BigAutoField primary-key convention and does not use UUID as primary key.
If a non-guessable identifier is required, it is implemented as a separate external_id UUID field rather than replacing the primary key.
Audit log records are persisted and generated through server-side write paths for create, edit, and status actions.
The new app is registered and routed without disturbing existing namespaces.
mandatory_tests: Add tests for model creation, server-side validation, audit-log generation, and route accessibility in apps.waiting_list.tests.
acceptance_criteria: The repository contains a new waiting_list app with migration-backed models and bounded route wiring.
Server-side validation covers required fields and masked-input-compatible formats for phone and date-of-birth.
The waiting list Entry model uses an int primary key consistent with repository conventions.

tasks.goal: Create the waiting list Django app foundation, schema, services, admin wiring, and route registration.
inputs: User-facing waiting list data model requirements
Existing Django app patterns in workorders and inventory
implementation_notes: Use the repository default int/BigAutoField primary-key convention for waiting list Entry.
Do not use UUID as primary key for Entry.

If the implementation encounters a concrete need for a non-guessable or externally shareable identifier, add a separate external_id UUIDField(default=uuid.uuid4, unique=True, editable=False) instead of replacing the primary key.
Keep service_id as a bounded string field with an application-local service catalog or choices, not a new relational model.
Centralize audit-log creation in service helpers so detail drawer actions and future writes share the same persisted timeline source.
Bound access to authenticated users only in this block.
deliverables: New waiting_list Django app with models, forms, services, views, urls, admin, and tests
Schema migration for waiting list models
escalate_if: Route wiring or migration conflicts require changes outside the declared file set
Required validation semantics imply a broader shared form or validation abstraction

slices.title: Waiting List Dashboard, Drawer, And Handoff
slices.goal: Implement the waiting list HTMX user experience, keyboard and masking enhancements, nav integration, and project handoff updates.
forbidden_moves: Do not introduce full-page reloads for flows that can remain HTMX-driven.
Do not move validation, audit generation, or status mutation logic into client-side-only code.
Do not widen this slice into unrelated app Python behavior beyond nav integration and shared styling touch-ups.
required_checks: Manual interaction and visual verification against drafts/waiting_list.html for dashboard filtering, sortable table behavior, drawer flow, quick status actions, timeline presentation, and keyboard shortcuts.
control_points: Filtering, sorting, and drawer rendering use HTMX partial updates and querystring-driven server state.
Quick status actions persist audit records and refresh the visible UI without leaving the dashboard.
Keyboard shortcuts and input masks are progressive enhancements that do not block normal form use.
mandatory_tests: Extend apps.waiting_list.tests with HTMX partial, sort, drawer, quick-action, and navigation coverage.
acceptance_criteria: The waiting list dashboard follows the mockup's interaction model within the shared project design system.
Create/detail drawer, live filters, sortable table, status actions, and timeline work without full-page reload.
PROJECT_HANDOFF documents the new app, route surface, and shared UI conventions added by this block.

tasks.goal: Build the waiting list dashboard and drawer UX, wire navigation, add progressive enhancement scripts, and update handoff documentation.
inputs: Waiting list domain app from slice-waiting-list-domain
Shared visual primitives from previous slices
implementation_notes: Use GET-based filters and HTMX partial targets for table and drawer updates so UI state stays bookmarkable and server-owned.
Implement quick status actions and timeline refreshes through Django views and services, then swap the relevant partials.
Keep phone and DOB masking as lightweight progressive enhancement compatible with server-side validation.
Add the waiting list entry point to the shared navigation only after the route exists.
deliverables: Waiting list dashboard templates and HTMX partials
Drawer interactions, keyboard shortcuts, and masking enhancements
escalate_if: HTMX interactions require widening write scope into unrelated app Python modules
A client-side enhancement would need a new asset pipeline or third-party bundler
